text.title: Agents Production Deployment — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `agents-production-deployment` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: All tools inherit from a typed BaseTool with Pydantic inputs. Untyped tools rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped tools cause silent arg coercion bugs at scale; typed base catches them at registration.

rule.statement: Agent config (model, tools, limits, env) lives in YAML with `${ENV_VAR}` substitution. Hard-coded production config rejected.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded config requires a redeploy for any change; YAML + env substitution allows runtime knobs.

rule.statement: Every LLM and tool call emits a structured JSON log row with (request_id, call_type, model, tokens, latency_ms, success).
rule.rationale: Without structured logs, post-incident analysis is grep-driven and unreliable; JSON enables aggregation.

rule.statement: External calls (LLM provider, tools) use exponential backoff retry + circuit breaker. Bare calls rejected.
rule.rationale: Transient failures + provider outages are routine; without retry+circuit the agent is brittle.

rule.statement: An eval harness runs in CI on every PR + nightly; measures (success_rate, latency_p95, iterations, $/task).
rule.rationale: Without eval, regressions ship; CI gate catches them before customers see.
